notes: Launched successfully after a uplay initial login and restart. Game loaded correctly all functions working. Unfortunately camera spins in clockwise direction until forward movement is used then camera acts normally. Similar situation to subnautica camera spinning issue experienced by a few (including me).
title: Assassin's Creed® Unity

notes: uPlay launches, Unity launches and plays with good performance. Two issues: occasional crash at opening menu screen, and using a Steam Controller causes camera spins.
title: Assassin's Creed® Unity

notes: Runs perfectly, performance and visuals are solid. However the controller triggers are getting mapped as the wrong axis and being used for camera control, causing the camera to spin as if a constant up, right input is present. Holding down the right trigger prevents this but clearly the axis are mis-mapped.
title: Assassin's Creed® Unity

notes: Doesn't run the first time you start uplay. You need to login on Uplay, then stop uplay, restart ACU from steam and then it opens. It runs well on my machine, although I didn't try controller controls.
title: Assassin's Creed® Unity

notes:
Runs, uplay starts (however separate for each game). As with all uplay games you need to start once, quit uplay and then re-run.

Game also does not exit as proton thinks uplay is the game, need to kill uplay after exiting.

Also cannot move refresh rate past 60hz but this is a proton-wide issue anyhow.

Most of the issues are with uplay and it sounds like a proton patch needs to create a symbolic link to a general uplay instance for each appid as well as fixing other issues.
title: Assassin's Creed® Unity

notes: Installs! Runs! Gets to the main menu! And even gets into the game! Unfortunately there appears to be a controller issue, the camera just spins constantly, axis are mismapped or something.
title: Assassin's Creed® Unity

notes:
Following steps:
I installed AC Black Flag on Steam via Proton 4.2.3. I start Black Flag via Steam. Uplay opens.
I choose AC Unity in the games library. Download + Install.
Start AC Unity.
I can even start the first mission with avg 30-60 fps. but many crashes. I keep looking for solutions.
title: Assassin's Creed® Unity

cpu: AMD Ryzen 5 2500U with Radeon Vega Mobile Gfx
notes: Disable Uplay overlay. It runs but at around 12-15 FPS, which is not ideal. This is on the dGPU (RX 560X), not the iGPU (Vega 8). While framerate issues are typically attributed to inefficiencies in either Wine/Proton or DXVK, this is unclear due to the game's notoriety for being problematic with regards to performance (among other things).
title: Assassin's Creed® Unity

notes: Played until amdgpu driver crashed. (Unrelated) Some rather obtrusive stuttering at some times, otherwise runs incredibly well.
title: Assassin's Creed® Unity

notes: Very playable. I couldn't close the game and it stopped responding to inputs after opening the UPlay overlay, I advise turning that off. But aside from that, it works pretty well. Tested with controller, worked fine.
title: Assassin's Creed® Unity

notes: Controller mapping for right trigger is messed up. The game reads the trigger as a joystick axis, causing the camera to move. You can try swapping the trigger endpoints in the Steam Controller Configuration dialogue and the results are ok but crouching is awkward.  Seems that the controller mapping should be fixable in Proton??
title: Assassin's Creed® Unity

notes: Gamepad issues resolved, game runs perfectly with very good performance under Proton 4.2-5.
title: Assassin's Creed® Unity

notes: Game won't even install.. Stuck at 99% and disk allocation.
